channel.title: Capital-Allocation on >_ John Januszczak | Technology & Innovation
channel.description: Recent content in Capital-Allocation on >_ John Januszczak | Technology & Innovation

item.title: Why the Yield Curve is the Pulse of Every Bank
item.author: john@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: A deep dive into the ‘gravitational constant’ of finance: the yield curve. Former UBX CEO John Januszczak explains how interest rate spreads drive banking physics and why inversions stall the engine of innovation.

item.title: Unlocking the Basel Framework: How Smart Compliance Drives Higher ROE
item.author: john@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: A technical overview of the Basel Framework’s mechanics and how shifting from standardized to internal risk models can optimize capital efficiency and boost Return on Equity (ROE).

item.title: The Bank for the Unbankable
item.author: john@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: The Business Development Bank of Canada is neither a traditional bank nor a typical venture capitalist. Built to serve entrepreneurs rejected by commercial lenders, BDC has quietly become one of the most powerful counter-cyclical institutions in the Canadian economy—and the country’s largest venture capital investor. This is the story of how a government-owned bank learned to profit from market failure while stabilizing innovation, startups, and small businesses through crises.

item.title: The Temporal Architecture of Alpha
item.author: john@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: An overlay on Alex Danco’s ‘Prediction’ thesis, arguing that business strategy is finally moving from a spatial ‘portfolio’ model to a temporal ‘stream’ model.
